name: 菲利普·格拉斯
brief: 格拉斯出生于巴尔的摩的一个犹太家庭，父亲经营唱片店——他从小在店里接触到各种音乐。他在茱莉亚音乐学院学习后，前往巴黎师从传奇教师纳迪亚·布朗热，又在印度与西塔尔大师拉维·香卡的合作中获得了决定性的灵感。回到纽约后，他为了维持创作一度以出租车司机和水管工为生。如今他是在世最常被演出的古典作曲家之一。
briefEn: Glass was born into a Jewish family in Baltimore; his father ran a record shop, and the boy grew up immersed in every kind of music. After studying at the Juilliard School he traveled to Paris to work with the legendary teacher Nadia Boulanger, then found decisive inspiration in India through his collaboration with sitar master Ravi Shankar. Back in New York, he drove taxis and worked as a plumber to support his composing. Today he is one of the most frequently performed living classical composers in the world.
contribution: 格拉斯是简约主义音乐（Minimalism）的三大创始人之一（与史蒂夫·赖希和特里·赖利齐名）。他用重复的音型、缓慢的和声变化和稳定的脉动创造了一种催眠般的音乐体验，彻底挑战了20世纪中叶「复杂至上」的学院派美学。他的「肖像歌剧」三部曲（《海滩上的爱因斯坦》等）重新定义了歌剧这一古老体裁。
contributionEn: Glass is one of the three founding figures of Minimalism, alongside Steve Reich and Terry Riley. Through repeating patterns, slowly shifting harmonies, and a steady pulse, he created a hypnotic musical experience that fundamentally challenged the mid-twentieth-century academic orthodoxy of "complexity above all." His Portrait Trilogy of operas (beginning with Einstein on the Beach) redefined the ancient art form of opera itself.
style: 格拉斯的音乐以「加法结构」（Additive Structure）为核心——一个简短的音乐图案通过不断添加或减少音符而逐渐变形。他的和声语言虽然简洁（以三和弦为主），但和弦的连接方式往往出人意料，创造出一种既熟悉又陌生的声音。他的音乐模糊了古典与流行、东方与西方、高雅与通俗的界限，具有强烈的跨界特质。
styleEn: At the heart of Glass's music is additive structure — a brief musical figure that gradually transforms as notes are added or subtracted. His harmonic language is deceptively simple (built largely on triads), yet the way chords connect is often unexpected, producing a sound at once familiar and strange. His music blurs the boundaries between classical and popular, Eastern and Western, highbrow and accessible, giving it a powerfully cross-genre character.

famousScores.name: 《海滩上的爱因斯坦》
famousScores.desc: 与罗伯特·威尔逊合作的歌剧，五小时无中场休息，改变了歌剧的定义
famousScores.descEn: An opera created with Robert Wilson — five hours with no intermission, redefining what opera can be
famousScores.detail:
《海滩上的爱因斯坦》（1975—1976）是格拉斯与视觉剧场艺术家罗伯特·威尔逊长达四年合作的结晶，1976年7月首演于法国阿维尼翁戏剧节，随后在欧洲各地巡演，同年在纽约大都会歌剧院演出两场——门票售罄，排队等候者绕场而列。这部歌剧没有传统意义上的情节，没有可解读的叙事，只有音乐、舞蹈、光影和数字的循环往复，构成一幅关于爱因斯坦与相对论的超现实冥想。

作品演出时间长达五小时，中途没有传统的幕间休息，但观众被事先告知可以随时自由出入。歌词几乎全部由数字（1，2，3…）和音符名称（do，re，mi…）构成，完全放弃了传统的语言叙事。格拉斯亲自率领菲利普·格拉斯乐团演奏电风琴，演唱者以无实义的音节演唱，整个演出构成了一种超越日常经验的时间艺术。

《海滩上的爱因斯坦》彻底颠覆了歌剧的定义，也标志着简约主义音乐从艺术小众走向国际视野的关键时刻。它至今仍是20世纪表演艺术史上最具颠覆性的作品之一，并于1976年的巡演后让格拉斯背上了巨额债务——他不得不重操旧业当水管工还债，这段经历成为音乐界的传奇故事。
famousScores.detailEn:
Einstein on the Beach (1975–76) is the product of four years of collaboration between Glass and theater visionary Robert Wilson. It premiered at the Avignon Festival in July 1976 and toured Europe before appearing at the Metropolitan Opera in New York later that year in two sold-out performances with lines stretching around the block. The opera has no conventional plot or interpretable narrative — only music, dance, light, and cycling numbers — composing a surrealist meditation on Einstein and relativity.

The work runs five hours without a traditional intermission, though audiences are told in advance they may come and go freely. The lyrics consist almost entirely of numbers (1, 2, 3…) and solfège syllables (do, re, mi…), abandoning linguistic narration altogether. Glass himself performed on the electric organ with his Philip Glass Ensemble while singers intoned meaningless syllables, creating a temporal art that transcends ordinary experience.

Einstein on the Beach completely overturned the definition of opera and marked the pivotal moment when Minimalism moved from a niche art movement into the international spotlight. It remains one of the most radical works in twentieth-century performance history — and the 1976 tour left Glass deeply in debt, forcing him back to plumbing work to pay his bills, a story that has become legend in the music world.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Glass Einstein on the Beach Philip Glass Ensemble 1993

famousScores.name: 《楚门的世界》配乐
famousScores.desc: 电影配乐杰作，完美捕捉了虚假世界的不安
famousScores.descEn: A masterful film score that perfectly captures the unease of an artificial world
famousScores.detail:
《楚门的世界》（1998）是格拉斯最广为人知的电影配乐之一。导演彼得·威尔（Peter Weir）选择格拉斯的简约主义音乐风格，是因为那种循环往复、略带机械感的音乐质感与电影的核心主题——一个被设计好的、反复播放的虚假世界——形成了完美的形而上契合。格拉斯的音乐既温暖又令人不安，既像是阳光下的日常生活，又像是一个永无终点的梦。

配乐中最著名的段落《楚门睡觉》（Truman Sleeps）已成为格拉斯最受欢迎的钢琴小品之一，无数钢琴学习者将其作为简约主义音乐的入门曲目。那种简单的琶音音型与缓慢游移的和声，创造出一种悬置于时间之外的静谧感，完美配合了影片中楚门被困于虚假现实的意象。

这部配乐的成功极大地扩展了格拉斯的听众群体——许多原本对严肃音乐毫无兴趣的观众通过这部电影第一次接触到了简约主义音乐，并产生了强烈的共鸣。它证明了简约主义音乐与当代影视叙事之间存在天然的亲和力，也为后来大量好莱坞电影采用简约主义风格配乐奠定了范例。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Truman Show (1998) is among Glass's best-known film scores. Director Peter Weir chose Glass's Minimalist idiom because its cyclically repeating, slightly mechanical quality creates a perfect metaphysical fit with the film's central theme — a designed, endlessly looping artificial world. Glass's music is simultaneously warm and unsettling, simultaneously everyday-in-sunlight and an endless dream.

The most celebrated passage, "Truman Sleeps," has become one of Glass's most beloved piano pieces; countless piano students use it as an entry point into Minimalist music. Its simple arpeggiated pattern over slowly shifting harmonies creates a stillness suspended outside ordinary time, perfectly matching the image of Truman trapped in a manufactured reality.

The score dramatically expanded Glass's audience — many listeners with no previous interest in serious music first encountered Minimalism through this film and responded with powerful emotion. It demonstrated a natural affinity between Minimalist music and contemporary cinematic storytelling, establishing a template that has since been followed by a great many Hollywood films.

famousScores.name: 《时辰》 The Hours
famousScores.desc: 奥斯卡提名配乐，为三个时空的弗吉尼亚·伍尔夫织就声音的网
famousScores.descEn: An Oscar-nominated score weaving a sonic web across three timelines linked by Virginia Woolf
famousScores.detail:
《时辰》（2002）的配乐是格拉斯电影音乐创作的顶峰之一，影片讲述了三个不同时代女性（弗吉尼亚·伍尔夫本人、1951年的一位家庭主妇和2001年的一位纽约女性）的故事，它们通过伍尔夫的小说《达洛维夫人》神秘地联系在一起。导演斯蒂芬·戴德利（Stephen Daldry）要求格拉斯的音乐能够无缝穿越三个时代，成为连接三条故事线的隐形纽带。

格拉斯的配乐以钢琴为核心，辅以弦乐和木管，创造了一种充满张力而又优雅的「时间之流」意象。他特意模糊了不同段落之间的界限——听者常常难以分辨一段音乐属于哪个时代，这种模糊性本身正是影片主题的音乐体现：时间是流动的，苦难与爱都超越时代。配乐在当年获得了奥斯卡提名。

《时辰》配乐展示了格拉斯的另一面——他不仅是重复循环音型的大师，也能以简约的旋律线条写出真正动人心弦的情感音乐。配乐中的《变奏曲》（Metamorphosis，部分取材自他的钢琴作品《变形记》）已成为独立的音乐会曲目，在世界各地的音乐厅被频繁演出。
famousScores.detailEn:
The score for The Hours (2002) stands among the peaks of Glass's film music. The film follows three women in different eras — Virginia Woolf herself, a 1950s housewife, and a New York woman in 2001 — mysteriously connected through Woolf's novel Mrs. Dalloway. Director Stephen Daldry asked for music that could move seamlessly across three periods, becoming the invisible thread binding the three narratives together.

Glass built his score around the piano with strings and woodwinds, creating an image of "the flow of time" charged with tension yet suffused with elegance. He deliberately blurred the boundaries between different passages — listeners often cannot tell to which era a given musical section belongs, and this ambiguity is itself the musical embodiment of the film's theme: time is fluid, and suffering and love transcend all periods. The score received an Academy Award nomination.

The Hours score reveals another side of Glass — not merely the master of cycling repetitive patterns but a composer capable of writing genuinely moving emotional music through spare melodic lines. The "Metamorphosis" passages (partly drawn from his piano work of the same name) have become independent concert pieces, performed frequently in concert halls around the world.

famousScores.name: 《Glassworks》
famousScores.desc: 六乐章的室内乐作品，简约主义的入门首选
famousScores.descEn: A six-movement chamber work — the ideal introduction to Minimalism
famousScores.detail:
《Glassworks》（1981）是格拉斯为CBS Masterworks唱片公司录制的专辑，专门为「新的听众群体」——那些对古典音乐感到陌生的年轻听众——而设计。六个乐章交替以「开放」（Opening）、「封闭」（Closing）为名，以钢琴、弦乐、双簧管和长笛的不同组合呈现，共同构成了一幅简约主义音乐的全景素描。

专辑封面由著名的设计师设计，整体视觉风格现代而简洁，与音乐本身的美学高度一致。格拉斯在策划这张专辑时明确意图：用平易近人的音乐语言，不妥协于商业流行，但也不拒绝更广泛的听众。专辑出版后大获成功，成为格拉斯最畅销的录音之一，也成为许多人接触简约主义音乐的第一扇窗。

《Glassworks》的意义不仅在于音乐本身——它也是古典音乐如何与当代媒体和市场结合的重要案例。它证明了严肃音乐可以在不降低艺术标准的前提下吸引大众听众，开创了后来许多古典音乐「跨界专辑」的模式。时至今日，其中的「Opening」一曲仍是全球使用最广泛的简约主义钢琴曲目之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
Glassworks (1981) was recorded for CBS Masterworks and designed specifically for a "new audience" — young listeners unfamiliar with classical music. Its six movements, alternating between pieces titled "Opening" and "Closing," use various combinations of piano, strings, oboe, and flute to compose a panoramic sketch of Minimalist music.

The album's cover, designed with a modern and spare visual aesthetic, perfectly matched the music's own sensibility. In planning the album Glass had a clear intention: accessible musical language, no compromise with commercial pop, but no refusal of a broader audience either. It became one of Glass's best-selling recordings upon release and opened the door to Minimalism for a great many listeners.

The significance of Glassworks extends beyond the music itself — it is an important case study in how classical music can connect with contemporary media and markets. It demonstrated that serious music can attract a mass audience without lowering its artistic standards, establishing a template for many subsequent "crossover" classical albums. To this day, the opening "Opening" remains one of the most widely used Minimalist piano pieces in the world.

famousScores.name: 《第三交响曲》
famousScores.desc: 弦乐交响曲，简约主义与交响传统的融合
famousScores.descEn: A symphony for strings, merging Minimalist language with the symphonic tradition
famousScores.detail:
《第三交响曲》（1995）是格拉斯迄今15部交响曲中最为凝练、最具说服力的一部。全曲为纯弦乐编制，四个乐章完全放弃了木管、铜管和打击乐，只用弦乐的丰富层次构建了一座精密的音乐建筑。这种极简的编制并未削减音乐的丰富性，反而使每一种弦乐音色都获得了最大限度的突出和雕刻。

这部交响曲体现了格拉斯从纯粹简约主义向更复杂、更具情感深度的风格转变。第一乐章以短促有力的弦乐齐奏开头，随后展开一系列层叠的复调织体；慢乐章中那种淡然而带有沉思色彩的弦乐长线条，明显受到巴洛克音乐线条美学的影响，但和声语言依然是格拉斯式的——清晰的三和弦在意想不到的方向流动。

《第三交响曲》的创作时期正值格拉斯系统探索交响曲体裁的阶段。他在接下来的数十年里陆续完成了超过10部交响曲，形成了20世纪末最重要的交响曲系列之一。这部第三交响曲因其编制的独特性和风格的成熟，常被视为这个系列的代表作，也是弦乐管弦乐文献中当代作品的重要新增。
famousScores.detailEn:
Symphony No. 3 (1995) is perhaps the most concentrated and persuasive of Glass's fifteen symphonies to date. The entire work is scored for strings alone — its four movements abandoning woodwinds, brass, and percussion entirely, constructing a precise musical architecture through the layered richness of strings alone. This ultra-spare scoring does not diminish the music's richness; on the contrary, it allows every string timbre to be maximally highlighted and sculpted.

The symphony embodies Glass's evolution from pure Minimalism toward a style of greater complexity and emotional depth. The first movement opens with sharp, forceful unison strings before unfolding a series of layered contrapuntal textures; the slow movement's long, meditative string lines are clearly influenced by Baroque melodic aesthetics, yet the harmonic language remains characteristically Glass — clear triads flowing in unexpected directions.

The Third Symphony was written during a period when Glass was systematically exploring the symphonic medium. Over the following decades he completed more than ten symphonies, forming one of the most important symphonic series of the late twentieth century. The Third, for its unique scoring and stylistic maturity, is frequently considered the flagship of this series, and an important contemporary addition to the string orchestral literature.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Philip Glass Symphony No.3 Dennis Russell Davies

famousScores.name: 《甘地》（萨蒂亚格拉哈）
famousScores.desc: 以甘地的南非岁月为题材，以梵文唱词与宏大简约主义著称
famousScores.descEn: A monumental Minimalist opera on Gandhi's South African years, sung entirely in Sanskrit
famousScores.detail:
《萨蒂亚格拉哈》（1979）是格拉斯「肖像歌剧三部曲」的第二部，以圣雄甘地在南非推行非暴力抵抗运动（「萨蒂亚格拉哈」意为「坚持真理」）期间的历史事件为蓝本。歌剧的唱词全部取自梵文版《薄伽梵歌》，歌手以梵文演唱——这对西方歌剧听众而言完全陌生的语言，在格拉斯看来具有「让语言成为纯粹声音」的美学效果。

三幕结构以时间倒置的方式呈现：第一幕处于甘地时代之后（托尔斯泰），第二幕是甘地时代（泰戈尔），第三幕处于甘地时代之前（马丁·路德·金）——三位精神先知共同构成了非暴力抵抗精神的历史谱系。首演于1980年在鹿特丹，随后在全球各大歌剧院上演，引发了广泛的文化讨论。

《萨蒂亚格拉哈》展示了简约主义音乐的宏大叙事潜力——它长达三小时，音乐以大型管弦乐队为基础，简约的和声重复在舞台上积累起史诗般的情感重量。它是格拉斯最重要的歌剧作品之一，也是20世纪政治题材歌剧的重要范例，证明了先锋音乐语言可以与宏大的人文主题完美融合。
famousScores.detailEn:
Satyagraha (1979) is the second installment of Glass's Portrait Opera Trilogy, drawing on Mahatma Gandhi's years in South Africa leading the nonviolent resistance movement — satyagraha means "truth-force" or "holding to truth." The entire libretto is drawn from the Sanskrit Bhagavad Gita, with singers performing in Sanskrit — a language entirely foreign to Western operatic audiences, which in Glass's view has the aesthetic effect of "making language into pure sound."

The three-act structure is presented in inverted chronology: Act I is set after Gandhi's era (Tolstoy), Act II in Gandhi's era (Tagore), Act III before Gandhi's era (Martin Luther King Jr.) — three spiritual prophets composing the historical genealogy of nonviolent resistance. The premiere in Rotterdam in 1980 was followed by productions in major opera houses around the world, generating wide cultural discussion.

Satyagraha reveals the epic narrative potential of Minimalist music. Running three hours, scored for a large orchestra, its simple harmonic repetitions accumulate on stage into an emotional weight of genuinely epic proportions. It is among Glass's most important operatic works and a significant example of politically engaged opera in the twentieth century, demonstrating that avant-garde musical language can fuse seamlessly with the grandest humanist themes.

famousScores.name: 《失衡生活》配乐（科亚尼斯卡西）
famousScores.desc: 无对白纪录片的革命性配乐，重新定义了音乐与图像的关系
famousScores.descEn: The revolutionary score for a wordless documentary — redefining the relationship between music and image
famousScores.detail:
《科亚尼斯卡西》（1982）是导演戈弗雷·里吉奥用十年时间独立制作的无对白、无叙述者的实验性纪录片，记录了现代工业文明与自然之间的冲突。「科亚尼斯卡西」是霍皮语，意为「失去平衡的生活」。里吉奥曾回忆，他最初找到格拉斯时根本没有预算——他只是带着剪辑好的影片画面找上门，问格拉斯是否愿意为它配乐。格拉斯被画面深深震撼，决定无偿完成这项工作。

格拉斯为影片创作的音乐彻底打破了「配乐从属于画面」的传统——在这部影片中，音乐与图像是平等的、互相解读的合作伙伴。那种循环往复、永不停歇的音乐节奏，配合延时摄影下的城市人潮，创造出一种令人窒息的「机器文明吞噬人性」的视觉-听觉体验。影片中以低音人声重复吟唱片名「Koyaanisqatsi」的段落，至今仍被认为是人声简约主义音乐的经典之作。

这部影片和配乐在商业上几乎一无所有（里吉奥几乎无法让影院院线接受这部没有任何传统叙事的影片），却在文化影响上极为深远。它重新定义了音乐与图像的关系，启发了后来MTV、视觉音乐和实验影像的整个创作路径。格拉斯后来又与里吉奥合作了两部续集（《波瓦卡西》和《纳古亚希》），形成了完整的「卡西三部曲」。
famousScores.detailEn:
Koyaanisqatsi (1982) is an experimental documentary that director Godfrey Reggio spent ten years making independently — no dialogue, no narrator, only images of the collision between modern industrial civilization and the natural world. "Koyaanisqatsi" is a Hopi word meaning "life out of balance." Reggio later recalled that when he first approached Glass he had essentially no budget — he simply arrived with his edited footage and asked whether Glass would be willing to score it. Glass was so deeply struck by the images that he agreed to do so at no charge.

The music Glass composed for the film shattered the conventional assumption that a film score is subordinate to the image. Here, music and image are equal, mutually interpreting partners. The endlessly cycling rhythms, paired with time-lapse footage of urban crowds, create a suffocating audio-visual experience of "machine civilization devouring human life." The passages in which a bass chorus repeatedly intones the word "Koyaanisqatsi" are still regarded as classics of vocal Minimalism.

The film and score achieved almost nothing commercially — Reggio could barely persuade theater chains to show a film with no conventional narrative — yet their cultural impact has been immense. They redefined the relationship between music and image and inspired the entire trajectory of later MTV aesthetics, visual music, and experimental cinema. Glass went on to collaborate with Reggio on two sequels (Powaqqatsi and Naqoyqatsi), completing the "Qatsi Trilogy."

famousScores.name: 《阿肯那顿》（歌剧）
famousScores.desc: 肖像歌剧三部曲的终章，以古埃及法老阿肯那顿的宗教改革为主题
famousScores.descEn: The final Portrait Opera — a hypnotic journey into the world of the monotheistic pharaoh Akhnaten
famousScores.detail:
《阿肯那顿》（1983）是格拉斯肖像歌剧三部曲的压轴之作，以古埃及法老阿肯那顿（约公元前1353—1336年在位）推行一神教改革的历史为素材。阿肯那顿废除了埃及传统的多神教，独尊太阳神阿顿，建立了人类历史上最早的一神教体系之一，死后其改革被彻底推翻。歌剧的唱词来自古埃及文、希伯来文和阿卡德文——三种死语言构成了一个关于历史、宗教和遗忘的冥想。

阿肯那顿的角色由反声男高音（countertenor）演唱，这种高而空旷的声音质感在舞台上制造出一种超凡脱俗的存在感，仿佛这位法老的灵魂真的游离于人间之外。格拉斯在这部歌剧中将他的音乐语言推向了最庄严、最缓慢的极端——整部歌剧几乎没有快速的节奏，如同一场石碑般沉重的时间仪式。

《阿肯那顿》1984年在斯图加特首演，随后迅速成为当代歌剧的保留曲目。2019年大都会歌剧院的新制作引发了轰动，使这部36年前的作品重新成为舆论焦点。它是格拉斯最重要的歌剧作品，以其对人类精神追求的深刻刻画和对时间感的彻底颠覆，在当代歌剧史上占据了不可撼动的地位。
famousScores.detailEn:
Akhnaten (1983) is the capstone of Glass's Portrait Opera Trilogy, drawing on the story of the Egyptian Pharaoh Akhnaten (reigned c. 1353–1336 BCE) and his radical monotheistic religious reform. Akhnaten abolished Egypt's traditional polytheism, declaring sole devotion to the sun-disk Aten and establishing one of the earliest monotheistic systems in human history; after his death his reforms were entirely reversed. The opera's libretto is drawn from ancient Egyptian, Hebrew, and Akkadian — three dead languages composing a meditation on history, religion, and oblivion.

The role of Akhnaten is sung by a countertenor whose high, ethereal vocal quality creates on stage a sense of otherworldly presence, as if the pharaoh's spirit truly floats apart from the human world. In this opera Glass pushed his musical language to its most solemn and slow extreme — the work contains almost no fast music, unfolding like a stone-heavy ritual in time.

Akhnaten premiered in Stuttgart in 1984 and quickly became a staple of contemporary opera programming. A new Metropolitan Opera production in 2019 caused a sensation, returning this thirty-six-year-old work to the center of critical attention. It is Glass's most important opera, its profound depiction of humanity's spiritual aspirations and its complete overturning of conventional temporal experience securing it an unshakeable place in the history of contemporary opera.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Philip Glass Akhnaten opera Dennis Russell Davies

famousScores.name: 《变形记》（钢琴独奏）
famousScores.desc: 卡夫卡的变形，简约主义最深刻的冥想之一
famousScores.descEn: Kafka's Metamorphosis refracted through Minimalism — one of Glass's most meditative piano works
famousScores.detail:
《变形记》（1988）是格拉斯最重要的独奏钢琴作品，由五首各自独立又相互关联的小品构成，灵感来自弗兰茨·卡夫卡的同名小说。格拉斯自述，他并非在「描绘」卡夫卡的故事情节，而是试图捕捉卡夫卡世界的情绪质感——那种既平静又充满内在紧张的氛围，如同梦境与现实之间的中间地带。

五首作品在风格上高度统一，以持续的分解和弦音型为基础，和声在小范围内缓慢游移，创造出一种时间仿佛停滞的悬置感。与格拉斯的许多作品相比，《变形记》更加内省、更加个人化——少了许多戏剧性的推进，多了一种近乎禅意的静观。

这部作品后来成为《时辰》（2002）电影配乐的素材来源之一，其中部分旋律以变奏形式出现在影片中。单独作为音乐会曲目，《变形记》是格拉斯最常被演奏的钢琴作品，也是简约主义钢琴音乐中最精美、最耐人寻味的篇章之一，以其深邃的冥想气质，将格拉斯带入了一种超越技法层面的精神维度。
famousScores.detailEn:
Metamorphosis (1988) is Glass's most important solo piano work, consisting of five independent but related pieces inspired by Franz Kafka's novella of the same name. Glass has stated that he was not "depicting" Kafka's plot but attempting to capture the emotional texture of Kafka's world — an atmosphere at once calm and charged with internal tension, like the borderland between dream and reality.

The five pieces are remarkably unified in style, built on continuous arpeggiated patterns over which harmonies slowly drift within a limited range, creating a sensation of time suspended. Compared to many of Glass's works, Metamorphosis is more inward and personal — less dramatic forward momentum, more of a near-Zen contemplative stillness.

The work later became a source of material for the score of The Hours (2002), with portions of its melodies appearing in varied form in the film. As a concert work in its own right, Metamorphosis is Glass's most frequently performed piano piece and one of the most beautiful and resonant works in the Minimalist piano literature — through its deep meditative quality, it carries Glass into a spiritual dimension that transcends questions of mere technique.

famousScores.name: 《十二乐章音乐》
famousScores.desc: 简约主义音乐的宏伟总结，格拉斯早期风格的百科全书
famousScores.descEn: The grand summation of Minimalism — an encyclopaedia of Glass's early style
famousScores.detail:
《十二乐章音乐》（1971—1974）是格拉斯早期创作生涯最重要的大型作品，也是简约主义音乐史上规模最宏大的实体——完整演出时长超过四小时，十二首乐章构成一个巨大的音乐宇宙。这部作品最初是一首单独的乐曲，格拉斯在演出后听到一位乐手说「还有十一首」（误以为将有续集），于是真的续写了十一首，构成这个史诗般的系列。

这部作品系统地展示了格拉斯「加法结构」的全部可能性——从最简单的单音线条到复杂的多声部对位，从纯粹的脉动律动到带有旋律起伏的长线条，每一章都在探索一个特定的音乐问题。在第十二章中，格拉斯开始引入泛调性和弦（超越了他最初的严格简约主义）——这标志着他向更广阔风格疆域的转变。

《十二乐章音乐》的演出本身是一种仪式性的文化事件：场地通常采用非传统剧场，听众被鼓励在演出过程中自由走动、休息、进食，如同一场长达数小时的音乐朝圣。它是格拉斯迄今创作的最能代表其早期激进精神的作品，也是简约主义运动最重要的单件作品之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
Music in Twelve Parts (1971–74) is the most important large-scale work of Glass's early career and the most monumental entity in the history of Minimalist music — in complete performance it lasts over four hours, with twelve movements composing a vast musical universe. The work began as a single piece; after a performance, Glass overheard a musician say "eleven more to go" (jokingly imagining sequels), and Glass actually composed eleven more, building this epic cycle.

The work systematically explores every possibility of Glass's additive structure — from the simplest single melodic line to complex multi-voice counterpoint, from pure pulsing rhythm to long melodic arcs, each movement investigating a specific musical question. In the twelfth movement Glass begins to introduce pan-tonal harmonies (moving beyond his initial strict Minimalism) — marking the beginning of his evolution toward a wider stylistic territory.

A performance of Music in Twelve Parts is itself a ritual cultural event: venues are typically non-traditional theaters, audiences are encouraged to move around, rest, and eat during the performance, as in a hours-long musical pilgrimage. It is the work in Glass's catalog that most fully embodies his early radical spirit, and one of the most important single works in the Minimalist movement.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Philip Glass Music in Twelve Parts Ensemble

influence: 格拉斯的影响远超古典音乐范畴。他的重复性结构影响了电子音乐（Aphex Twin、Autechre）、后摇滚（Sigur Rós、Mogwai）和影视配乐（汉斯·季默的许多手法源自简约主义）。他证明了严肃音乐可以拥有广泛的听众——他的音乐会吸引大量非古典音乐听众。他与拉维·香卡、大卫·鲍伊等跨界合作，打破了古典音乐的文化藩篱。
influenceEn: Glass's influence extends far beyond classical music. His repetitive structures have shaped electronic music (Aphex Twin, Autechre), post-rock (Sigur Ros, Mogwai), and film scoring (many of Hans Zimmer's techniques are rooted in Minimalism). He proved that serious music can command a broad audience — his concerts regularly draw large numbers of listeners with no background in classical music. His cross-genre collaborations with Ravi Shankar, David Bowie, and others broke down the cultural barriers surrounding the concert hall.
